reforms.description: Japan announced measures to promote inward direct investment by removing unnecessary regulations and implementing policies to reduce start-up costs for new foreign investors, including low-interest loans and extended tax incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers and costs for foreign investors, the initiative is expected to enhance competition and innovation within the Japanese market.
reforms.status_evidence: Japan announced measures to promote inward direct investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Japan's approach to foreign investment, aiming to enhance the competitive landscape and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In July 1995, Japan announced measures to promote inward direct investment by removing unnecessary regulations and by implementing policies to reduce the start-up costs for new foreign investors.
